channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Crimson Desert's latest update adds more bosses for rematches as well as new unarmed combat skills for Damiane
item.content:encoded:
The Crimson Desert team only recently released an update which allowed you to mount iguanas and other fantastic beasts, but it is already back at it, releasing a new patch which, among other things, adds more bosses to the rematch system meaning you can fight them again and again.

These additional encounters come as part of Crimson Desert's 1.07.00 update, which is available now across Steam (PC and Mac), PlayStation, Xbox and Epic Games Store. I am afraid that those playing via the Mac App Store will have to wait a little longer for this update, but developer Pearl Abyss has assured it is in-progress.

As for those rematches, their locations can be found by heading to your in-game journal, and then flicking through the Knowledge section through to Memory Fragments and then onto Bosses. I won't spoil the details here, but you may come across some bosses in areas who weren't there before.

In addition, today's Crimson Desert update has added a new unarmed combat skill-tree for Damiane, one of the game's additional player characters. Just like with Oongka's own unarmed combat update in the last patch, this can be found by selecting 'Fists' in the skill menu. Both Damiane and Oongka also now have the option to do an aerial stab, which sounds very Assassin's Creed.

Meanwhile, Pearl Abyss was clearly not content with its last mount-infused patch, and has now added additional wolf and bear types which can be registered to ride. The team has also added reins for certain mounts, though it did not specify which ones.

You can check out the full patch notes for Crimson Desert Version 1.07.00 below, while a list of known issues can be found via the game's website.

New Additions:

  • Added more bosses that can be challenged again in rematches.
  • [Damiane] Added unarmed combat skills - You can find the new skills by selecting "Fists" in the skill menu.
  • [Damiane/Oongka] Added "Aerial Stab" for Damiane and Oongka.
  • [Kliff] Added "Blinding Flash Finisher" for unarmed combat.
  • Added additional wolf and bear types that can be registered as mounts.
  • Added reins for certain mounts.

Content

  • [Damiane] Fixed an issue where the "Elegant Carmine Leather Armor" appeared abnormally.
  • Fixed an issue where the "Faces on the Bounty Notice" could not be progressed if the player obtained Ulzok's bounty notice before speaking with Yann.
  • Improved visual effects of "Champion's Plate Gloves."
  • Fixed an issue where the "Eastern Witch's Fan" would disappear when extracted.
  • Fixed an issue where the Kuku Bird Egg would occasionally disappear from the nest.
  • Fixed an issue where some NPCs would appear abnormally at the Saltroad Trading Post.
  • Fixed an issue where the "Check the honey quality at the Pollengarth Apiary" could not be completed for "Ugmon's Request."
  • Fixed an issue while carrying out the "Protectors of the Abyss" quest, where tokens inserted into the door would disappear if only some of them had been inserted and the player moved too far away.
  • Fixed an issue where the "Plentiful Greeting" quest could not be progressed even when the relevant requirements were met.
  • Fixed an issue where floppy-eared bulldogs could not equip the "Sigil of Valor".
  • Fixed an issue where Ibexes could not be sold to a fence.
  • Fixed an issue where missions could not be progressed if comrades were disbanded after moving to the mission location while assembled.
  • Fixed an issue where legendary mounts could not be summoned when switching to Kliff in certain situations.
  • Fixed an issue where the A.T.A.G. could not be summoned in certain situations.
  • Fixed an issue where the A.T.A.G. would occasionally not appear in the hangar.
  • Increased the number of animals in the world that can be used as mounts.

Controls

  • Fixed an issue where the eating animation would not be canceled by other actions while unarmed on Easy or Normal difficulty.
  • Fixed an issue where the handwheel on the coil pillar could only be interacted with from certain angles.

Combat / Action

  • [Oongka] Added a charge phase to Explosive Strike.
  • [Oongka] Improved chaining into "Stab" when dual-wielding.
  • [Damiane] Improved general movement animation while unarmed.
  • Improved most unarmed attacks and skills so that they can now successfully hit enemies lying on the ground.

UI

  • Fixed an issue where the icon for the "Sword of Starlight" differed from its actual appearance.
  • Fixed an issue where, when playing with a controller, certain key guides shown in the bottom-right area of the screen would display keyboard and mouse inputs.

Graphics & Settings

  • Fixed a GPU crash issue that occurred with AMD driver version 26.5.1.

Localisation

  • Fixed various localisation errors and improved localisation quality across all languages.

Others

  • Fixed an issue where, when the "Display Melee Weapons" option was set to "Selected Only", the sheath for a two-handed sword would be displayed while a one-handed sword and shield were equipped.
  • Fixed an issue where equipment equipped on horses would disappear in certain situations.
  • Fixed an issue where lions, tigers, and large wolves would fall over every time they were hit.
  • Fixed an issue where other actions could be performed while attacking on horseback.
  • Fixed an issue where two-handed cannons would flicker while sliding.
  • Fixed an issue where the player would glide an excessive distance during a jump attack while equipped with a sword and shield in certain situations.
  • Applied bug fixes and improvements regarding specific bosses.

Crimson Desert has been a huge success despite middling reviews - "A vast world and even vaster array of MMO-like activities mix with glittering fidelity in Crimson Desert, but what good is it without much character, texture or charm?" asked Eurogamer's Crimson Desert review. In just five days, Crimson Desert hit 3m sales, and then only a week later Pearl Abyss announced Crimson Desert had sold a further 1m copies. This success reportedly saw Pearl Abyss give every employee a bonus that equated to about $3,400.

Earlier this week, Pearl Abyss announced a fourfold increase in operating revenue compared to this time last year, thanks in no small part to Crimson Desert. It subsequently suggested some Crimson Desert DLC could be on the way.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/crimson-desert-patch-boss-rematches-damiane-unarmed-combat
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Subnautica 2 already has huge concurrent player counts and has swum to 2 million sales
item.content:encoded:
Subnautica 2 has done exceptionally well following its launch into Early Access, quickly coasting to 467,000 concurrent players on Steam alone.

The much-anticipated sequel to the oceanic survival series released in the later-half of yesterday, the 14th March. Quickly, a wave of players took to the game, hitting a peak of 467,582 concurrents on Steam before tapering off in the evening.

Official Subnautica social media accounts announced the game had sold over 1m copies across all platforms - which include PC as well as Xbox Series X/S. While writing this article, Unknown Worlds announced it had passed 2m sales within 12 hours of launching. The game is also available on Game Pass, though figures for how popular it is there aren't publicly known.

For reference, the original Subnautica reached a lifetime peak of 51,156 concurrent players on Steam years ago, so there's a substantial increase in excitement around the sequel. Its follow-up Subnautica: Below Zero had 42,115 peak concurrent players roughly five years ago.

Things are also looking good when comparing Subnautica 2 side-by-side with its peers in the survival genre. Rust, the juggernaut of the genre, hit an all-time PC peak concurrent player count of 262,284, while the recent pirate plunder hit Windrose came in at 222,134. The only recent survival release that has come close to those numbers is Dune: Awakening, which had a similarly explosive launch last year.

This, obviously, is great news for Subnautica 2 developer Unknown Worlds. Not only because a popular release is good for any game development studio, but because publisher Krafton reportedly agreed to a hefty bonus package tied to the game's performance - one that Unknown Worlds leadership claimed was at the heart of legal tussles some time ago.

So it's a darn good start for Unknown Worlds and Subnautica 2 as it begins its Early Access journey. Now of course the pressure is on to see if Unknown Worlds can maintain strong momentum as it sales (or perhaps dives) into the rest of the year. If you're keen to find out what Subnautica 2 is like, you can read Eurogamer's Subnautica 2 preview for some insight into what's on offer with the game's initial release.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/subnautica-2-sales-launch-one-million
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
